segment_edps: I can't predict pipeline past 45 days and my board keeps asking.
I hired 3 SDRs and none of them are producing.
My champion at our biggest account just left.
input.brief_angles: Pipeline predictability post-raise; SDR ramp failure

variant_01.subject: Pipeline at {{COMPANY_NAME}} post-raise
variant_01.body: {{FIRST_NAME}} - been in the seat you're in. 45-day pipeline visibility is the thing that kept me up. Most Series B teams we see hit a lag around day 60 when the new SDRs haven't ramped yet. Worth comparing notes on what actually moved the number?

variant_02.subject: {{COMPANY_NAME}}'s SDR ramp
variant_02.body: Hey {{FIRST_NAME}}, hired 3 SDRs myself and only one made it past month 4. Seeing the same pattern across ~12 Series B teams this year. Happy to share what the ones who kept their hires did differently.

segment_edps: Enterprise deals keep slipping and I don't know why.
My AEs can't articulate the platform story.
Procurement cycles eat 6 weeks I didn't plan for.
input.brief_market: Series B fintechs, VP Sales role

variant_01.subject: Enterprise slip at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_01.body: {{FIRST_NAME}} - every VP Sales at a fintech I talk to is losing 6+ weeks to procurement they didn't plan for. Usually shows up as Q-end slippage. Curious if that's on your radar at {{COMPANY_NAME}}.

variant_02.subject: AE platform pitch at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_02.body: Hey {{FIRST_NAME}}, when my AEs couldn't articulate the platform story, deals died in stage 3. Watched it happen to 4 fintechs this year before we fixed the enablement stack. Worth 15 min if that's familiar.

segment_edps: My sales team is 100% inbound and the pipe is flat.
I don't know what a working outbound motion looks like for us.
I've been burned by 2 outbound agencies already.
input.brief_angles: First outbound motion for inbound-only teams

variant_01.subject: Outbound at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_01.body: {{FIRST_NAME}} - been in the chair where inbound plateaus and nobody on the team has ever run outbound. Usually the first 60 days are a mess. What did work for 3 similar B2B services shops was starting with 1 segment, not 5. Worth a quick swap?

variant_02.subject: {{COMPANY_NAME}}'s 3rd try at outbound
variant_02.body: Hey {{FIRST_NAME}}, if {{COMPANY_NAME}} has already been through 2 outbound vendors, the 3rd pitch probably reads like noise. Not trying to sell - just saw the hiring post and wanted to flag what the 2 shops in your range who made it stick did differently.

input.skill_text: [campaign-copywriting SKILL.md - 5-variable personalization, challenger angle when given prior output]
segment_edps: I can't predict pipeline past 45 days.
I hired 3 SDRs and none are producing.

variant_01.subject: {{RECENT_POST_TOPIC}} at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_01.body: Hi {{FIRST_NAME}}, saw your post on {{RECENT_POST_TOPIC}}. Most {{INDUSTRY}} founders we work with end up solving it by tightening the ICP before adding SDRs. Curious if that's where you're leaning.

variant_02.subject: {{COMPANY_NAME}} + {{CASE_STUDY_COMPANY}}
variant_02.body: {{FIRST_NAME}}, {{CASE_STUDY_COMPANY}} was in your spot post-Series-B - hit {{CASE_STUDY_METRIC}} by rebuilding the SDR playbook around accounts, not leads. Happy to share the 3-slide summary.

segment_edps: Enterprise deals slip.
Procurement cycles surprise me.

variant_01.subject: Enterprise slip at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_01.body: {{FIRST_NAME}} - every VP Sales at a fintech...

variant_01.subject: {{PRESS_HEADLINE}} + the 6-week gap
variant_01.body: {{FIRST_NAME}}, after {{PRESS_HEADLINE}} the procurement drag usually gets worse, not better. {{CASE_STUDY_COMPANY}} cut theirs from 6 weeks to 3 by pre-running security review. Worth a look?

variant_02.subject: Enablement at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_02.body: Hi {{FIRST_NAME}}, pulled a pattern across 8 Series-B fintechs: AE enablement gaps show up as 23% higher slip rate in stage 3. Can share the diagnostic if useful.

segment_edps: Pipe is flat.
Been burned by outbound agencies.

variant_01.subject: {{HIRING_ROLES}} hire at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_01.body: Hi {{FIRST_NAME}}, the {{HIRING_ROLES}} posting at {{COMPANY_NAME}} usually means inbound has plateaued. We helped {{CASE_STUDY_COMPANY}} ({{CASE_STUDY_CUSTOMER_TYPE}}) go from 0 to {{CASE_STUDY_METRIC}} in {{CASE_STUDY_TIMEFRAME}} without adding headcount first.

variant_02.subject: {{COMPANY_NAME}}'s ICP gap
variant_02.body: {{FIRST_NAME}}, before building an outbound motion, most B2B services teams skip the ICP tightening step. {{CASE_STUDY_COMPANY}} recovered their 3rd agency attempt by doing that one thing first. 10 min swap?

segment_edps: Marketing could be better.

variant_01.subject: Attribution at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_01.body: {{FIRST_NAME}} - hard to write a real opener without knowing the specific pain, but the CMOs I talk to lose 20% of budget to attribution gaps they can't see. If that's familiar at {{COMPANY_NAME}}, worth a swap.

variant_02.subject: Channel mix at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_02.body: Hey {{FIRST_NAME}}, the mid-market CMO pattern I keep seeing: channel mix looks fine on the dashboard, underperforming on revenue. Usually a segmentation issue, not a spend one. Quick chat?

segment_edps: Growth is slow.

variant_01.subject: Growth lever at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_01.body: {{FIRST_NAME}} - the founders I talk to who were stuck at slow growth usually had a distribution problem, not a product one. If that sounds like {{COMPANY_NAME}}, happy to compare notes.

variant_02.subject: Early-stage distribution
variant_02.body: Hey {{FIRST_NAME}}, at the early stage the thing that kills growth is defaulting to the founders' network. Worked with 4 similar companies who unstuck by switching channels. 15 min swap?

segment_edps: Developers adopt but don't convert.
Self-serve to enterprise handoff is broken.

variant_01.subject: PLG to sales at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_01.body: {{FIRST_NAME}} - every devtools founder I've worked with hit the same wall at the self-serve to enterprise handoff. Usually it's the CS team catching deals that should've been an AE. Worth comparing notes?

variant_02.subject: {{COMPANY_NAME}}'s conversion gap
variant_02.body: Hey {{FIRST_NAME}}, dev adoption without conversion is the most common devtools pain I see at Series A. Three companies we worked with fixed it by putting PQL scoring before outbound. Happy to share the framework.

segment_edps: I can't tell if my AEs are underperforming or the market is.
Forecasting is a guess.

variant_01.subject: AE diagnostics at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_01.body: {{FIRST_NAME}} - hardest call I had to make as Head of Rev was firing an AE in a down market. Built a diagnostic to stop guessing. 3 similar Series C teams use it now. Worth a look?

variant_02.subject: Forecasting at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_02.body: Hey {{FIRST_NAME}}, the forecasting-as-a-guess pattern at Series C usually traces back to inconsistent stage definitions, not AE performance. Happy to share what 4 teams shipped to fix it.

segment_edps: Attribution is broken.
My stack is too complex.

variant_01.subject: {{STACK_MARKETING}} + attribution
variant_01.body: Hi {{FIRST_NAME}}, saw {{COMPANY_NAME}} runs {{STACK_MARKETING}}. The attribution gap we see most often is at the {{STACK_CRM}} handoff. {{CASE_STUDY_COMPANY}} recovered 22% visibility by simplifying that one link.

variant_02.subject: Stack audit at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_02.body: {{FIRST_NAME}}, mid-market marketing ops teams average 14 tools; the top performers run 7. Happy to share our audit framework if the stack feels too complex.

segment_edps: I can't close the books on time.
Revenue recognition is manual.

variant_01.subject: Close cycle at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_01.body: {{FIRST_NAME}} - close cycles at fintech Series B usually break at rev rec, not at AR. Three CFOs I talked to last quarter shipped fixes in 60 days. Worth a swap?

variant_02.subject: Manual rev rec at {{COMPANY_NAME}}
variant_02.body: Hey {{FIRST_NAME}}, the manual rev rec pattern gets exponentially worse past $15M ARR. Happy to share what {{CASE_STUDY_COMPANY}} did to automate without a full stack rebuild.
